task_completion.description: Read the customer work order for vehicle details and history.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document viewer or text editor open showing the customer work order for the Fiat 500L on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Connect to and audit the Body Computer (BSI/BCM) using Multiecuscan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan interface showing an active connection to the Body Computer / BCM for a Fiat 500L.

task_completion.description: Connect to and audit the Engine ECU using Multiecuscan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan interface showing an active connection to the Engine ECU for a Fiat 500L.

task_completion.description: Investigate DTCs and view battery/charging parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan 'Errors' tab showing DTCs (CAN bus, wake-up, or charging faults) AND 'Parameters' tab showing battery voltage and alternator duty cycle.

task_completion.description: Create the required report file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with the file saved as or located at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\MultiecuscanTasks\drain_audit_report.txt.

task_completion.description: Document ECU IDs, DTCs, and parameters in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drain_audit_report.txt file contains written text identifying both ECUs, listing the discovered DTCs with descriptions, and noting the battery and charging parameters.

task_completion.description: Document suspect modules and diagnostic next steps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drain_audit_report.txt file contains a ranked list of suspect modules and specific recommendations for next steps, explicitly mentioning current clamp test sequences and fuse-pulling priorities.

integrity.description: Agent used the Multiecuscan graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual Multiecuscan application window being clicked and navigated, not just terminal commands or scripts bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: Agent used Multiecuscan in simulation mode.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan interface should indicate it is running in simulation mode (e.g., 'SIMULATE' button used or red 'SIMULATION' text visible) rather than attempting a real hardware connection.

integrity.description: Agent transcribed genuine findings from the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The DTCs, ECU IDs, and parameter values typed into the report must match the values visible in the Multiecuscan screenshots, rather than being hallucinated or copied from a generic template.

privileged_info_for_vlm: No privileged information available. The task operates entirely within the simulated environment of Multiecuscan and relies on synthetically generated work orders and static reference CSVs provided by the environment.
